text.title: Lookalike and Retargeting Audience Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Lookalike and Retargeting Audience Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Seed = top-decile by revenue or engagement, ≥1,000 records. Smaller seeds produce noisy lookalikes; lower-quality seeds amplify bad fit.
rule.rationale: Seed quality dominates lookalike performance; investing in seed = highest-ROI knob.

rule.statement: Every audience has explicit exclusion: current customers (unless cross-sell), 14-day site visitors, recent purchasers. Without exclusion, money on already-converted users.
rule.rationale: Without exclusion, 20-40% of retargeting spend wastes on already-converted users.

rule.statement: Lookalike degree 2-5% (FB/Google's percentage axis). <2% = too narrow; >5% = noisy. Start at 2%, expand if quality holds.
rule.rationale: Empirical industry data: 2-5% is the productive range; degree shifts decay quality fast.

rule.statement: Monthly audit of audience overlap. Overlap >30% between audiences = duplicate spend; consolidate or differentiate.
rule.rationale: Audience overlap silently drains budget; monthly audit catches it before quarterly review.

rule.statement: Each audience has a frequency cap (e.g., 7 impressions / 7 days). No cap = ad fatigue + brand damage.
rule.rationale: Industry research: ad fatigue triggers brand erosion past 7-10 impressions/week.
